missing_data_notes: Less than 5 annual datapoints available for 5-year trend analysis.
Less than 3 annual datapoints available for 3-year trend analysis.
Less than 4 quarterly datapoints available for recent quarter trend analysis.

signals.evidence_text: We are subject to the risk of public health crises such as pandemics, earthquakes, adverse weather conditions, other natural disasters, terrorism, geopolitical conflict, other physical security threats, power loss, cyber-attacks, and other catastrophic events and crises.

We are involved in numerous lawsuits, including stockholder derivative lawsuits and putative class action lawsuits, many of which claim statutory damages and/or seek significant changes to our business operations
our ability to add and retain users and maintain levels of user engagement with our products
Any future declines in the size of our active user base may adversely impact our ability to deliver ad impressions and, in turn, our financial performance.
In addition, Apple has released changes to iOS that limit our ability, and the ability of others in the digital advertising industry, to target and measure ads effectively.

we must manage the potential conflicts between locally accepted business practices in any given jurisdiction and our obligations to comply with laws and regulations, including anti-corruption laws or regulations applicable to us, such as the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and the U.K. Bribery Act 2010
We face design, manufacturing, and supply chain risks with respect to our consumer hardware products that, if not properly managed, could adversely impact our financial results

We are currently subject to securities litigation in connection with our platform and user data practices and the misuse of certain data by a developer that shared such data with third parties in violation of our terms and policies; the disclosure of our earnings results for the second quarter of 2018; a former employee's allegations and release of internal company documents beginning in September 2021; and the disclosure of our earnings results for the fourth quarter of 2021. We may experience more such litigation following future periods of volatility. Any securities litigation could subject us to substantial costs, divert resources and the attention of management from our business, and adversely affect our business.

Delaware law and provisions in our certificate of incorporation and bylaws could make a merger, tender offer, or proxy contest difficult, thereby depressing the trading price of our Class A common stock.

We incur significant expenses in operating our business, and some of our investments, particularly our investments in our artificial intelligence initiatives as well as Reality Labs, have the effect of reducing our operating margin and profitability. If our investments are not successful longer-term, our business and financial performance will be harmed.

The dual class structure of our common stock has the effect of concentrating voting control with our CEO and certain other holders of our Class B common stock; this will limit or preclude your ability to influence corporate matters.
Changes in tax laws or tax rulings, or changes in interpretations of existing laws, could materially affect our financial position, results of operations, and cash flows
fluctuations and declines in the size of our active user base in one or more markets from time to time
users feel that their experience is diminished as a result of the decisions we make with respect to the frequency, prominence, format, size, and quality of ads that we display

signals.subheading: Change in Accounting Estimate + 3 more + 1 more
signals.evidence_text: The increase was almost entirely driven by advertising revenue.
signals.judge_rationale: States that advertising revenue increased mainly due to ad performance improvements, indirectly related to data handling and security.

signals.evidence_text: Our advertising revenue has been, and we expect will continue to be, adversely affected by reduced marketer spending as a result of limitations on our ad targeting and measurement tools arising from changes to the regulatory environment and third-party mobile operating systems and browsers.
signals.judge_rationale: Addresses the risk that regulatory and legislative developments could adversely affect advertising revenue, aligning with cybersecurity and privacy concerns.

signals.evidence_text: Apple made certain changes to its products and data use policies in connection with changes to its iOS operating system that reduce our and other iOS developers' ability to target and measure advertising, which has negatively impacted, and we expect will continue to negatively impact, the size of the budgets marketers are willing to commit to us and other advertising platforms.
signals.judge_rationale: Specifically mentions how changes in data use policies (e.g., in iOS) negatively impact ad targeting, with implications for cybersecurity and privacy.

signals.evidence_text: We expect these and other developments will have further impact in the future. As a result, we have implemented, and we will continue to implement, whether voluntarily or otherwise, changes to our products and user data practices, which reduce our ability to effectively target and measure ads and may negatively impact our advertising revenue and user engagement.
signals.judge_rationale: Highlights ongoing regulatory changes that could reduce ad targeting and measurement effectiveness, influencing privacy and cybersecurity risks.

signals.subheading: Change in Accounting Estimate + 3 more + 1 more
signals.evidence_text: In January 2025, we completed an assessment of the useful lives of certain servers and network assets, which resulted in an increase in their estimated useful life to 5.5 years, effective beginning fiscal year 2025. Based on the servers and network assets placed in service as of December 31, 2024, we expect this change in accounting estimate will reduce our full-year 2025 depreciation expense by approximately $2.9 billion.
signals.judge_rationale: Discusses a change in accounting estimates affecting depreciation expense, which relates to the risk of financial misestimation but not directly to cybersecurity or privacy.

signals.subheading: Foreign Exchange Impact on Revenue + 8 more + 1 more
signals.evidence_text: Changes in foreign exchange rates had an unfavorable impact on our revenue in the full year 2024 compared to the same period in 2023.
signals.judge_rationale: Addresses the impact of foreign exchange rate changes on revenue, which is a financial risk but not directly linked to cybersecurity or privacy.

signals.evidence_text: We believe our ongoing improvements to ad targeting and measurement are continuing to drive improved results for advertisers. However, we expect that some of these efforts will be long-term initiatives, and that the legislative, regulatory and platform developments described above will continue to adversely impact our advertising revenue for the foreseeable future.
signals.judge_rationale: Indicates that regulatory and platform development efforts, aiming to improve ad targeting and measurement, could have long-term impacts on advertising revenue, with privacy and security implications.

signals.evidence_text: The increase in costs and expenses was mainly due to increases in operational expenses related to our data centers and technical infrastructure and employee compensation
signals.judge_rationale: Supports the risk related to increasing operational expenses due to data centers, technical infrastructure, and employee compensation which could impact cybersecurity and privacy through increased data processing and infrastructure complexity.

signals.subheading: Change in Accounting Estimate + 3 more + 1 more
signals.evidence_text: Our advertising revenue is generated by displaying ad products on Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and third-party mobile applications. Marketers pay for ad products either directly or through their relationships with advertising agencies or resellers, based on the number of impressions delivered or the number of actions, such as clicks, taken by users.
signals.judge_rationale: Describes the source of advertising revenue through ad products, relevant to privacy and security due to data collection practices.

signals.subheading: Change in Accounting Estimate + 3 more + 1 more
signals.evidence_text: The increase in average price per ad in 2024 was driven by an increase in advertising demand, which we believe is mainly due to ongoing improvements to our ad performance from our ad targeting and measurement tools.
signals.judge_rationale: Supports that increased ad demand, driven by better ad targeting/measurement, relies on data practices affecting privacy and security.

signals.subheading: Foreign Exchange Impact on Revenue + 8 more + 1 more
signals.evidence_text: Our effective tax rate in 2024 decreased compared to 2023, primarily due to excess tax benefits recognized from share-based compensation and an increase in research tax credits.
signals.judge_rationale: Mentions a decrease in effective tax rate due to tax benefits, relevant to financial stability but not specifically privacy/security risks.

representative_signals.evidence_text: We are subject to the risk of public health crises such as pandemics, earthquakes, adverse weather conditions, other natural disasters, terrorism, geopolitical conflict, other physical security threats, power loss, cyber-attacks, and other catastrophic events and crises.

We are involved in numerous lawsuits, including stockholder derivative lawsuits and putative class action lawsuits, many of which claim statutory damages and/or seek significant changes to our business operations
our ability to add and retain users and maintain levels of user engagement with our products
Any future declines in the size of our active user base may adversely impact our ability to deliver ad impressions and, in turn, our financial performance.
In addition, Apple has released changes to iOS that limit our ability, and the ability of others in the digital advertising industry, to target and measure ads effectively.
The increase was almost entirely driven by advertising revenue.

Our advertising revenue has been, and we expect will continue to be, adversely affected by reduced marketer spending as a result of limitations on our ad targeting and measurement tools arising from changes to the regulatory environment and third-party mobile operating systems and browsers.

Apple made certain changes to its products and data use policies in connection with changes to its iOS operating system that reduce our and other iOS developers' ability to target and measure advertising, which has negatively impacted, and we expect will continue to negatively impact, the size of the budgets marketers are willing to commit to us and other advertising platforms.

We expect these and other developments will have further impact in the future. As a result, we have implemented, and we will continue to implement, whether voluntarily or otherwise, changes to our products and user data practices, which reduce our ability to effectively target and measure ads and may negatively impact our advertising revenue and user engagement.

In January 2025, we completed an assessment of the useful lives of certain servers and network assets, which resulted in an increase in their estimated useful life to 5.5 years, effective beginning fiscal year 2025. Based on the servers and network assets placed in service as of December 31, 2024, we expect this change in accounting estimate will reduce our full-year 2025 depreciation expense by approximately $2.9 billion.
